expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (109 lines) and architecture.md (57 lines) both contain substantive content

Selected: Modular Monolith. Justified by simplicity (#1 priority), solo developer, minimal budget. Microkernel considered but rejected because compliance domain doesn't naturally decompose into core+plugins.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Table in Step 2 ranks all 8: Service-Based, Microkernel, Modular Monolith, Layered, Event-Driven, Microservices, Space-Based, Service-Oriented

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: 4 structural FFs: No circular module dependencies, Module boundary enforcement (cross-module access only through public API), Single deployment artifact, Database schema per module (no cross-module direct table access). All are structural invariants of a modular monolith.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (modular monolith)
expectations.evidence: FFs enforce modular-monolith-specific invariants: module boundary enforcement (no internal imports across modules), circular dependency prevention, single deployment artifact (not applicable to microservices), schema-per-module. These differ fundamentally from microservices FFs.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach column
expectations.evidence: Tool/Approach column present: dependency-cruiser/ArchUnit, Package visibility rules/lint rules, Build script check, SQL analysis or ORM config check

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 2 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
